{{Infobox character
| name = Omega
| parents = {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Omega is Aila Escalon's father}}
| children = {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila Escalon]], Jak}}
| abilities = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|dissolving his body into a gray mass, moving through Subspace, regenerate from gray ooze, [[Subspace Drive]], [[enchantment-breaking gun]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|, [[contract magic]], folding into himself}}{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|, unique Subspace warp}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Grave Hound]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|, [[World Spirit]], founder of Escalon}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Last Horizon]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|, [[Escalon Company]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|, [[Titan Knights]]}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[assassin]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|, [[Pilot|pilot]], mercenary, hired murderer}}
| birthplace = {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zykaros system]]}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]]}}
| appears_in = The Last Horizon
| introduced = [[The Captain (novel)|The Captain]]
}}

''See also: [[Omega Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Omega is an assassin for hire, also known as the [[Grave Hound]], who operates under a set of personal principles despite his chaotic and unpredictable nature. He possesses a highly versatile physiology, capable of shifting between a human form—resembling a man in his forties with dark hair, a trimmed beard, and an orange [[Aethertech]] replacement eye—and a dark, shapeshifting substance such as goo or biomass. This organic composition allows him to regenerate by consuming organic matter, sprout multiple eyes or mouths at will, and flow through ship wreckage. While he is considered a villain who might kill for amusement or profit, he has also been known to defend the powerless. He is a contract worker who maintains various enterprises and has connections to several individuals, including [[Raion]], [[Sola]], and a nephew. Omega is also capable of resisting the influence of [[Subspace]] for extended periods.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Known as the [[Grave Hound]], Omega is a famous galactic assassin and saboteur who has lived for at least one hundred and fifty standard years. Appearing as a forty-year-old man with dark hair, short beard, and an orange [[Aethertech]] prosthetic eye embedded in a [[steel]] frame, he possesses an [[Aethertech body]] and an internal [[Subspace Drive]]. He can dissolve his form into a gray mass to move through or store matter in [[Subspace]], allowing him to regenerate from ooze using a limited stockpile of mass. After escaping a ship's prison by using a colorless copy of himself, the prisoner was eventually relocated to a new holding facility. During his exploits, he has used an enchantment-breaking gun that fires energy-trailing bullets capable of piercing starship armor, and he has even been known to devour others to drag them through [[Subspace]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">39</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is a being capable of dissolving his body into a gray mass and moving through [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10">''The Captain'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]]'s human form appears to be a forty-year-old man with dark hair and a short beard.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] has an [[Aethertech]] prosthetic left eye that shines orange.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is the [[Grave Hound]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is a character who can emerge from [[Subspace]] in human form.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c12">''The Captain'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] possesses a cybernetic eye that blazes orange.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] has an [[Aethertech body]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] can regenerate from gray ooze.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] uses [[Subspace]] to store excess matter for regeneration.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c15">''The Captain'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] possesses an internal [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] has an [[Aethertech eye]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] can dissolve his body to slither away.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is the name used by the narrator to address the assassin.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c23">''The Captain'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] can pop in and out of [[Subspace]] at will.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c23" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]]'s regeneration is fueled by a limited stockpile of available mass.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c23" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is a potential entity responsible for distracting [[Magic Tower security]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c23" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is an attacker who uses a gun and fires bullets that trail energy.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c24">''The Captain'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]]'s bullets are capable of piercing normal starship armor.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] has a patch of [[steel]] surrounding his [[Aethertech eye]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c25">''The Captain'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is an assassin who calls himself the [[Grave Hound]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c26">''The Captain'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is at least a hundred and fifty standard years old.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c26" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]]'s left eye is an [[Aethertech]] prosthetic embedded into a metal frame.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c26" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is a character being kept locked up.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c26" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is a character who spread information about the [[Captain of The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c27">''The Captain'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is a prisoner who escaped from the ship's prison using a colorless copy of himself.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c29">''The Captain'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] has orange eyes, one brighter than the other.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]]'s regeneration is not infinite.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] was relocated to a new holding facility.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is also referred to as [[Grave Hound]] or [[Omal]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c40">''The Captain'', chapter 40</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is willing to attack a [[Hive]] for one hundred thousand standards.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is a famous assassin throughout the galaxy known for sabotage and assassination.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]]'s goal is to go after [[Solstice]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c41">''The Captain'', chapter 41</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] is unable to pull a trigger when attempting to shoot the narrator.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c41" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]]'s body turns into gray goo when shot by [[Sola]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c41" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] can chuckle in three different voices at once.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c41" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] can devour others and drag them through [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c46">''The Captain'', chapter 46</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] can take a human form.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] was hiding in the shadows during the encounter.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c47">''The Captain'', chapter 47</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] used an enchantment-breaking gun that flashed orange.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c48">''The Captain'', chapter 48</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Omega is an immortal, chaotic yet rational crew member and pilot who serves on the [[Zenith]] Starship under a contract bound by magic. Formerly kept in a [[vault]] jar and known as a hired murderer for the [[Escalon Company]], this ooze-man possesses [[Aethertech]] modifications fused to his flesh and can morph his dark gray biomass into human-like forms, mouths, or even weapons. While he has previously hijacked vessels and attacked [[Varic Vallenar]], he is currently restricted against murder by his contract and seeks to disable [[Solstice]]. Throughout his service, Omega utilizes various abilities such as folding into himself to vanish, communicating through feeds, and using his orange [[Aethertech eye]] to scan his surroundings.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">41</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a crew member who is described as chaotic and rational.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c2">''The Engineer'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is the [[Pilot]] of the crew and wants to defeat [[Solstice]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c5">''The Engineer'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a character capable of pushing [[Aethertech]] beyond its limits.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c7">''The Engineer'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a crew member on [[The Last Horizon]] who has [[Aethertech]] modifications fused with his flesh.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9">''The Engineer'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] was previously kept in a jar in the [[vault]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a character on the ship who can be kicked out.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a character that [[Raion]] can relieve of his post.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is receiving medical treatment.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10">''The Engineer'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]]'s request is to disable [[Solstice]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c18">''The Engineer'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] questions if the new [[Engineer]] is allowed six requests.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a member of the crew.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is the hired murderer of the [[Escalon Company]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c22">''The Engineer'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is immortal.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] can spread dark gray biomass to form faces.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] views [[Raion]] as a fun challenge to unravel his persona.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is the source for [[Subspace Drives]] in the envisioned fleet.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24">''The Engineer'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a character who eats during the flight to his destination.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c27">''The Engineer'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] has gray ooze flowing from his back that forms mouths.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] can assemble a gun from his dark ooze.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] has both a human eye and an artificial eye.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is capable of vanishing by folding into himself.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a dark ooze that can morph into a human-like form.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c28">''The Engineer'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] can communicate through a feed.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c28" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a pilot on the [[Zenith]] Starship.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29">''The Engineer'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] possesses scanners.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30">''The Engineer'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a member of the crew who is bound by a contract.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a gray ooze with an orange eye.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] uses a treadmill.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is bound by [[contract magic]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c34">''The Engineer'', chapter 34</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] has an [[Aethertech eye]] that blazes orange in excitement.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c34" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is a mercenary who previously attacked [[Varic Vallenar]] at the [[Magic Tower]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] suggests killing [[Starhammer]] or someone else.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c41">''The Engineer'', chapter 41</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Advocates]] have contact with [[Omega]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c43">''The Engineer'', chapter 43</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] can melt into ooze.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c43" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] has a restriction against murder reinforced by [[contract magic]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45">''The Engineer'', chapter 45</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] hijacked a series of vessels and then abandoned them in space.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50">''The Engineer'', chapter 50</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] can pull a gun out of his [[Subspace]] storage.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] knows that the narrator's students found him once.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51">''The Engineer'', chapter 51</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] is an ooze-man.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] can use lasers to remove cuffs.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] can ooze into wounds.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c54">''The Engineer'', chapter 54</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omal]], known as Omega, is an experienced man with at least a century of expertise who serves as a crew member aboard [[The Last Horizon]]. Utilizing [[Aethertech]] eyes and guns stored within a [[Subspace inventory]], he communicates via [[Subline]] connection and operates various ship systems including the [[Pilot]] console, scanners, and the [[Subspace Drive]]. His body is partially composed of a dark gray ooze that allows him to partially liquefy, slither away, and sprout eyes from fifteen tendrils. During an away team mission on enemy ships, he was partially destroyed with only his top-right quarter remaining intact, yet he later demonstrated the ability to evade debris and enemy fire while moving through a worm's body. Clad in an orange-and-white [[Titan Knight]] combat suit, he interacts with the [[Captain]] and can be heard chuckling in half a dozen different voices.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">34</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a crew member who communicates via [[Subline]] connection.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3">''The Knight'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] uses a unique [[Subspace warp]] to travel.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a crew member who uses [[Aethertech]] eyes and guns.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] uses [[Subspace]] to store his guns.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a character that can slither away.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4">''The Knight'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] vanished.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a member of the crew and sits at an orange-lit wedge of the table.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c8">''The Knight'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] can sprout eyes from fifteen tendrils of dark gray ooze.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] can keep [[Horizon]] company.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c14">''The Knight'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is aboard [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c15">''The Knight'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is someone who needs to be woken up.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17">''The Knight'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] operates the [[Pilot]] console.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c18">''The Knight'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Sola]] lost eyes on [[Omega]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] was on the ship's console surrounded by bodies.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a character who can partially liquefy himself and sprout eyes.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c22">''The Knight'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a character who was being transmitted audio to.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c23">''The Knight'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a character on the ship who can dance onto the bridge.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c23" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]]'s real name is [[Omal]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c23" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is someone [[Varic]] orders to be contacted.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c28">''The Knight'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a character who can link himself to the [[Pilot]] console.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c28" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] was dancing from foot to foot.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35">''The Knight'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a crew member who operates a console.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36">''The Knight'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]]'s body is partially composed of a dark gray ooze that can reform.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a man with rare [[Aethertech]] and at least a century of experience.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] has a [[Subspace inventory]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is part of an away team sent aboard enemy ships.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c37">''The Knight'', chapter 37</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] was partially destroyed, with only the top-right quarter of his body remaining intact.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c37" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is a crew member who operates scanners and the [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38">''The Knight'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] chuckles in a half-dozen different voices.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is capable of evading debris and enemy fire while moving through a changing gap in a worm's body.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator saved [[Omega]] for last.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c40">''The Knight'', chapter 40</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] wears an orange-and-white uniform which is the combat suit of the [[Titan Knights]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42">''The Knight'', chapter 42</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] refers to the narrator as [[Captain]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Omega]] is an entity that the narrator interacts with.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c43">''The Knight'', chapter 43</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|An assassin for hire from the [[Zykaros system]], Omega is a combatant who utilizes an optical implant and [[Aethertech]] to function. Known as the [[Grave Hound]] and the founder of Escalon, he is a shapeshifting entity capable of manifesting as black ooze or a human form by using dark goo to hold himself in place. As the [[Pilot]] of [[The Last Horizon]] and the [[World Spirit]], he can manipulate subspace to store his mass, pull weapons from another dimension, and even enhance a ship's thrusters. Despite being the father of [[Aila Escalon]] and grandfather to [[Jak]], he has fought his own daughter and possesses a clone of her. Throughout his various assignments for [[Benri Vallenar]], he has demonstrated extreme regenerative abilities, allowing him to reconstitute himself after being shot or even surviving an explosion by wrapping himself in a missile.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">62</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a combatant who uses an optical implant to provide feeds.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c3">''The Pilot'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] was standing over a bloody body.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] has a mouth that forms on a tendril extending from his shoulder.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c4">''The Pilot'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] chews gum and spins a handgun.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is kept in a secure [[vault]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8">''The Pilot'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a character capable of manifesting in the form of black ooze.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can manifest in a human form using dark goo to hold himself in place.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is [[Aila Escalon]]'s father.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14">''The Pilot'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] has a form of regeneration.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is not aboard the ship.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15">''The Pilot'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can sprout new mouths.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a device or entity used for sensors.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16">''The Pilot'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is located inside [[Shadow Ark]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] pooled on the ceiling of the [[Divine Titan]]’s cockpit.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18">''The Pilot'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a being capable of oozing across ceilings and manifesting his upper body.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can manifest a tongue from his finger.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can keep most of his mass tucked away in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24">''The Pilot'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] has another mouth on the side of his neck.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] has taken control of the cargo ship.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25">''The Pilot'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a character who operates a starship.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is an admitted assassin for hire.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] speaks in several voices at once.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c28">''The Pilot'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] has eyes on stalks.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29">''The Pilot'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can resist the call of [[Subspace]] longer than a normal human.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a pilot who can manifest multiple pairs of hands.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] helped the [[Pilot]] escape an encirclement by the defenders of [[Fathom]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30">''The Pilot'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is the [[Pilot]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is the [[World Spirit]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] fought his daughter.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can reconstitute himself after being shot.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] fought [[Aila]] and the [[Zenith Blade]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a [[Pilot]] who has been described as an insane killer.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]]'s knuckles bleed black goo.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can pull a glowing orange gun from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can fold himself into darkness and disappear into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is the [[Pilot]] of [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] has completed discreet assignments for [[Benri Vallenar]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a character who spoke about [[Solstice]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is known as the [[Grave Hound]] and was the founder of Escalon.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c34">''The Pilot'', chapter 34</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a valuable source of information against the enemy.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c35">''The Pilot'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The narrator is tasked with finding [[Omega]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c35" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can melt into goo and flow through wall channels to escape.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c36">''The Pilot'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]]'s supply of mass responds sluggishly when low.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c36" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a character who infiltrated [[Benri]]'s peers by disguising himself as an underling.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c38">''The Pilot'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is from the [[Zykaros system]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40">''The Pilot'', chapter 40</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a crew member who can roll six extra eyes.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] lost his daughter, family, reputation, and fifty years of his [[life]] to [[Solstice]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] wears a mask.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a provisional crew member.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] uses [[Aethertech]] in his ears.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c41">''The Pilot'', chapter 41</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a passenger on the shuttle and acts as the helm.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c42">''The Pilot'', chapter 42</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is [[Jak's grandfather]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c42" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] has a metallic eye that can expand.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c42" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is someone [[Aila Escalon]] has fought.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c44">''The Pilot'', chapter 44</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila Escalon]] is the daughter of [[Omega]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46">''The Pilot'', chapter 46</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] has a clone of his daughter.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c47">''The Pilot'', chapter 47</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can enhance the thrusters of [[The Last Horizon]] as the [[Pilot]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c47" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can dissolve into black goo.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c47" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] can wrap himself in a missile to blow up an [[Iron Hive]] and pull himself back together.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c48">''The Pilot'', chapter 48</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is a character who would find it hilarious if he died for the ruin of his own home planet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c48" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] previously stated that a stronger contract eats a weaker contract.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c48" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] was gone.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c49">''The Pilot'', chapter 49</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“Good evening, Black Syndicate lady. I’m the Grave Hound.”<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“Omega is being supplied with the best treatment I can manage in Medical.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“Let’s kill him! Or someone else, I’m not picky.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c41" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|“You have time to talk, Captain?” Omega asked over our Subline connection.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|"You think he told me? He wished me good luck and slithered away."<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon characters]]
[[Category:Assassins]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
